content:
From: Peter <cl-isabelle-users@lists.cam.ac.uk>
Subject: [isabelle] "Type variable has two distinct sorts" exception on locale interpretation

Dear list,

I have a strange exception when instantiating a locale, that seems to be
caused by accidentally overlapping type-variable names. The gist of it
is that interpretations with different type-name annotations cause
different (or no) exceptions.

In the original theory, I have also encountered cases where the
instantiation does not raise an exception, but yields a too special
type, by unrelated type variables with the same sort constraints being
unified.

A full example to reproduce the problem is below, here's the important
lines:

(*  Interpretation seems to depend on type-variable names
*)
interpretation foo: on_from_basic_indexable
  "basic_assn"
  "basic_extr"
  . (* Type variable has two distinct sorts, ?'a llvm_rep vs marker *)
  oops

interpretation foo: on_from_basic_indexable
  "basic_assn :: 'xxa::llvm_rep option list ⇒ 'xxa ptr ⇒ llvm_amemory ⇒
bool"
  "basic_extr"
  . (* Type variable has two distinct sorts, ?'a len2 vs marker *)
  oops

interpretation foo: on_from_basic_indexable
  "basic_assn :: 'xxa::llvm_rep option list ⇒ 'xxa ptr ⇒ llvm_amemory ⇒
bool"
  "basic_extr :: 'xxa ptr ⇒ 'xa::len2 word ⇒ 'xxa ptr llM"
  . (* works *)

In the example, I have used the marker type-class to pinpoint the
culprit inside the locale, that gets accidentally unified with some type
variables from the instantiation arguments (see below). It stems from a
definition statement inside the locale, that is subsequently used as
argument of a sublocale.

Any ideas what happens here? I'm currently trying to invent random type
names to work around that problem in my actual (more complicated) setup.

Best,

Peter

======================================================

Full example:

theory Scratch imports Main
begin

(* Constants to strip down example *)

class llvm_rep =
  fixes llrep :: "'a ⇒ nat"

instance nat :: llvm_rep by standard

class len2 =
  fixes len2 :: "'a ⇒ nat"

instance nat :: len2 by standard

class marker =
  fixes marker :: "'a ⇒ unit"

instance nat :: marker by standard

datatype 'a word = WORD nat

datatype 'a llM = LLM 'a

typedecl llvm_amemory
type_synonym ll_assn = "llvm_amemory ⇒ bool"

datatype 'a ptr = PTR nat

instance ptr :: (llvm_rep) llvm_rep by standard

consts
  list_assn :: "('a ⇒ 'c ⇒ ll_assn) ⇒ 'a list ⇒ 'c list ⇒ ll_assn"
  option_assn :: "('a ⇒ 'c ⇒ ll_assn) ⇒ 'a option ⇒ 'c option ⇒ ll_assn"
  oelem_assn :: "('a ⇒ 'c ⇒ ll_assn) ⇒ 'a option ⇒ 'c ⇒ ll_assn"

assn_comp :: "('a ⇒ 'b ⇒ ll_assn) ⇒ ('b ⇒ 'c ⇒ ll_assn) ⇒ ('a ⇒ 'c ⇒
ll_assn)" (infixr ‹O⇩a› 75)

(*
  Example: locale and sublocale with definitions
*)
locale gen_onindexed =
  fixes C :: "('xa ⇒ 'xc::llvm_rep ⇒ llvm_amemory ⇒ bool) ⇒ 'xa option
option list ⇒ 'xcc::llvm_rep ⇒ llvm_amemory ⇒ bool"
    and extr :: "'xcc ⇒ 'xsize::len2 word ⇒ 'xc ptr llM"

locale obasic_indexable =
  fixes A :: "'xa::llvm_rep option list ⇒ 'xc::llvm_rep ⇒ ll_assn"
    and extr :: "'xc ⇒ 'xsize::len2 word ⇒ 'xa ptr llM"

locale on_from_basic_indexable = basic: obasic_indexable basic_assn
basic_extr
  for basic_assn :: "'xc::llvm_rep option list ⇒ 'xcc::llvm_rep ⇒ ll_assn"
  and basic_extr :: "'xcc ⇒ 'xsize::len2 word ⇒ 'xc ptr llM"
begin

(**** I've introduced the marker type-class to pinpoint the culprit
of the accidental name identification, and it seems to be this 'xa, that
doesn't appear in the locale parameters, that is accidentally unified
with types from the locale instantiation *)

definition  assn :: "('xa::marker ⇒ 'xc ⇒ llvm_amemory ⇒ bool) ⇒ 'xa
option option list ⇒ 'xcc ⇒ llvm_amemory ⇒ bool"
    where "assn A ≡ list_assn (option_assn (oelem_assn A)) O⇩a basic_assn"

definition "extr ≡ basic_extr"
  sublocale gen_onindexed assn extr .

end

(*
  Triggering the problem:
*)

consts
  basic_assn :: "'a::llvm_rep option list ⇒ 'a ptr ⇒ ll_assn"
  basic_extr :: "'a::llvm_rep ptr ⇒ 'l::len2 word ⇒ 'a ptr llM"

(*
  Interpretation seems to depend on type-variable names
*)
interpretation foo: on_from_basic_indexable
  "basic_assn"
  "basic_extr"
  . (* Type variable has two distinct sorts, ?'a llvm_rep vs marker *)
  oops

interpretation foo: on_from_basic_indexable
  "basic_assn :: 'xxa::llvm_rep option list ⇒ 'xxa ptr ⇒ llvm_amemory ⇒
bool"
  "basic_extr"
  . (* Type variable has two distinct sorts, ?'a len2 vs marker *)
  oops

interpretation foo: on_from_basic_indexable
  "basic_assn :: 'xxa::llvm_rep option list ⇒ 'xxa ptr ⇒ llvm_amemory ⇒
bool"
  "basic_extr :: 'xxa ptr ⇒ 'xa::len2 word ⇒ 'xxa ptr llM"
  . (* works *)
